threads.threadKey: email-germaine santon - thank you and follow up
threads.subject: Germaine Santon - Thank you and Follow up

messages.body:
Dear Germaine,

Thank you again for taking the time to meet with me today. As always, I
appreciate your thoughtful questions and the diligence you bring to
evaluating any strategy before moving forward. That approach serves you
well.

Our discussion helped further clarify your primary objective: reducing and
ultimately eliminating the future tax burden associated with your IRA while
maintaining simplicity, flexibility, and peace of mind.

As we discussed, there is no rush to make a final decision before you have
had an opportunity to fully review the information and conduct your own due
diligence.
Action ItemsOur Team

*1. Charitable Strategy Information*

   - Kaelee will send the NDA directly to you via email so you can review,
   sign, and return it at your convenience.
   - Once received, we will provide the complete charitable strategy
   documentation, legal opinions, program materials, and supporting
   information for your review.

*2. Carbon Credit Information*

   - We will provide access to the carbon credit materials, including:
      - Program overview
      - Legal and tax opinion documentation
      - Treasury and IRS guidance currently available
      - Supporting due diligence materials

*3. Roth Conversion Analysis*

   - I will prepare updated projections showing:
      - No action (maintaining the current IRA structure)
      - Partial Roth conversion
      - Full Roth conversion
      - Potential tax impacts and mitigation opportunities
      - Future RMD comparisons

*4. Income and Asset Positioning Options*

   - I will also prepare information regarding the Allianz strategies and
   S&P Futures Index approach we discussed so you can evaluate them separately
   from the Roth conversion decision itself.

Your Action Items

*1. Review the Charitable Strategy Materials*
Review the documents and note any questions or concerns.

*2. Review the Carbon Credit Materials*
Determine whether you are comfortable continuing your due diligence on this
strategy.

*3. Continue Thinking About Conversion Timing*
Consider whether your preference is:

   - A larger conversion completed in one year, or
   - A phased conversion over two years.

There is no need to decide immediately, but your comfort level with the
pace of implementation is an important consideration.
Our Next Meeting

We are currently scheduled to meet:

*Friday at 1:00 PM*

During that meeting we will focus on:

   - Roth conversion alternatives
   - Tax mitigation opportunities
   - Charitable strategy review
   - Carbon credit due diligence questions
   - Comparing implementation paths side-by-side

Our goal will be to help you determine which approach best aligns with your
desire for simplicity, tax efficiency, and long-term financial flexibility.

Germaine, I know how important it is to you to avoid unnecessary complexity
and surprises. We will continue to move at a pace that allows you to fully
understand each option before making any decisions.

Please let me know if any questions arise before our next meeting.

Also, Jamie asked me to thank you for your kind words and prayers regarding
her father. They are greatly appreciated.

Warm regards,

Ken Ring
Kenneth E Ring Jr (Ken)
Managing Partner (CEO) | CPA
Certified Tax & Business Advisor (CTBA)
Certified Defense Financial Manager (CDFM)  Level III
Colonel (Ret), US Army | M.S. Strategic Studies
Paradigm Capital Group | paradigmcapitalfl.com
Email me at:  kring@paradigmcapitalfl.com
Business Cell: 239-470-8523
Book an Appointment at: pcgappointments.com

messages.subject: Re: Please assist client

messages.body:
Germaine,

Below is the link to our Dynamic Carbon Credits Client Portal, DNA Harmony
Client Portal and our BoxHouse Client Portal. Within the portal, you’ll
have access to video walkthroughs, the DCC Intake Form, DNA Harmony Intake
Form, BoxHouse Intake Form and all due diligence materials related to the
programs.


*Dynamic Carbon Credits Client Portal
<https://docsend.com/view/s/6rh4qdsg3p3jjmnx>*

*DNA Harmony Client Portal <https://docsend.com/view/s/edx3d4r6nuwptv4b>*

*BoxHouse Client Portal <https://docsend.com/view/s/4hkx8gvfxrf7iheu>*


The first time you open the link, you’ll be prompted to sign a one-time
NDA. Once signed, you’ll retain continuous access to all of our portals
using the email address you signed in with. If you have already signed our
NDA from viewing other strategy portals, you will not need to sign
anything. Thank you for respecting these measures.


*DNA Harmony*

Participants in this opportunity are able to buy a Series LLC holding DNA
Vibe gift cards that were acquired at a bulk volume discount. Choosing the
donation path, their Series LLC donates that property to a qualified
501(c)(3) charity as a cash-equivalent contribution at the fair-market
value. The result is a charitable donation amount that can be larger than
the participant’s cash outlay (e.g., $50k in creates about a $250k
donation).

   - *Minimum Contribution*: $50k
   - *Types of Income*: Works for all income types, subject to Limitations
   for Cash Donations (60% of AGI) and the OBBBA Updates for Charitable
   Contribution (.5% AGI Floor and 2/37ths Rule).



*BoxHouse*

The BoxHouse opportunity with Highland Steel allows buyers to purchase
BoxHouse foldable housing units—durable, mobile structures that can be
deployed quickly in disaster zones or remote work sites and generate income
while in use. Each unit is owned through a Series LLC/Trust structure set
up for the buyer, with Highland Steel acting as the exclusive distribution
and managing partner. Because the units qualify as equipment with VINs,
buyers are able to take accelerated tax deductions, including 100% bonus
depreciation under current federal law, alongside any operating income.

   - *Types of Income*: Works for all active income types, with certain
   types subject to the Excess Business Loss Limitation (W2, Retirement
   Income, etc.).

*Unit Breakdown*:

   - MiniBox: $70k Down payment, $350k FMV purchase price (Bonus
   Depreciation Deduction)
   - Bungalow: $100k down payment, $500k FMV purchase price (Bonus
   Depreciation Deduction)
   - Duplex: $130k down payment, $650k FMV purchase price (Bonus
   Depreciation Deduction)


Kenneth E Ring Jr (Ken)
Managing Partner (CEO) | CPA
Certified Tax & Business Advisor (CTBA)
Certified Defense Financial Manager (CDFM)  Level III
Colonel (Ret), US Army | M.S. Strategic Studies
Paradigm Capital Group | paradigmcapitalfl.com
Email me at:  kring@paradigmcapitalfl.com
Business Cell: 239-470-8523
Book an Appointment at: pcgappointments.com
Complete Your Confidential Questionnaire;  pcgsecurelink.com

Dear Germaine,

messages.body:
Hi Germaine,

Quick reminder for your call tomorrow at Thursday, June 4, 2026 2:30 PM.

You originally booked this to go over your Roth conversion and how to reduce the tax impact before making a move.

A lot of people miss this strategy and wind up overpaying.

We’ll make sure you have clarity before that.

Join here:


Talk soon,
Jaime

Ken and Jamie

threads.threadKey: email-germaine santon - next steps and information for our upcoming meeting
threads.subject: Germaine Santon - Next Steps and Information for Our Upcoming Meeting

messages.body:
Hi Germaine,

Thank you again for taking the time to speak with me. I really appreciate
how thoughtful and involved you are in managing your finances—it’s clear
you’ve already made a number of strong decisions, especially around your
Roth strategy and planning ahead for taxes.

From our conversation, I understand that your biggest focus right now is
making sure you’re not exposed to unnecessary taxes down the road, while
also maintaining control and avoiding unnecessary risk. That’s exactly the
kind of situation where a structured review can be very valuable.

For our next step, I’ll begin organizing a clear picture of your current
position so we can look at:

   - how your tax exposure may evolve over time,
   - how your current strategy is working,
   - and where there may be opportunities to improve outcomes without
   increasing risk.

*What we will do:*

   - Build an initial model of your current financial structure
   - Prepare a clear projection of future taxes and RMD impact

*What we need from you:*

   - You’re welcome to use our secure online questionnaire to gather your
   data, which imports directly into our Circle of Wealth software:
   pcgsecurelink.com
   - Alternatively, if you prefer, you can simply bring your latest
   statements to our next meeting and we can update everything together for a
   more precise present position and after-tax cash flow projection
   - If it’s easier, you can also upload any documents or statements to
   your secure folder here:
   https://www.dropbox.com/request/xtolj4pngkh2q9l0ug5p

*What happens next:*

   - We are scheduled to meet at *2:00 PM Eastern Time*. I will send a
   separate calendar invite with the details.
   - During that meeting, we’ll review everything together in a clear,
   side-by-side format
   - No decisions—just clarity and comparison

We’ll take this one step at a time so it feels manageable and fully under
your control.

If any questions come up in the meantime, please don’t hesitate to reach
out.

Looking forward to continuing the conversation.

Best regards,
Ken

Kenneth E Ring Jr (Ken)
Managing Partner (CEO) | CPA
Certified Tax & Business Advisor (CTBA)
Certified Defense Financial Manager (CDFM)  Level III
Colonel (Ret), US Army | M.S. Strategic Studies
Paradigm Capital Group | Global Financial Impact (GFI)
Email me at:  kring@paradigmcapitalfl.com
Business Cell: 239-470-8523
Book an Appointment at: pcgappointments.com
Complete Your Confidential Questionnaire;  pcgsecurelink.com

threads.threadKey: email-germaine santon - thank you and next steps
threads.subject: Germaine Santon - Thank you and Next Steps

messages.body:
Hi Germaine,

Thank you again for spending time with Jamie and me on Friday. We truly
appreciate how thoughtful and engaged you’ve been throughout this process.
It’s very clear that you’ve put a tremendous amount of care and discipline
into your financial planning and the decisions you’re making moving forward.

Our conversation helped us better understand not only your concerns
surrounding future taxes and Roth conversion planning, but also the
charitable and legacy goals that are important to you — particularly your
desire to honor your husband’s memory in a meaningful way through
charitable giving and potential scholarship planning.

We also appreciated the additional detail you shared regarding:

   - your Roth conversion goals and timing considerations,
   - your charitable giving philosophy and QCD strategy,
   - your donor advised fund structure,
   - your scholarship and Red Cross ideas,
   - and the private investment structure you’ve been researching.

That additional context is extremely helpful as we prepare analysis and
modeling tailored specifically to your goals, priorities, and comfort level.

Between now and our next meeting, Jamie and I will:

   - model several Roth conversion approaches,
   - review the potential tax implications and opportunities associated
   with those strategies,
   - and evaluate how charitable planning may best integrate into your
   overall tax and legacy objectives.

As discussed, our next meeting is confirmed for:

Friday, May 15, 2026
2:00 PM Eastern Time

We’ll send a separate calendar invite as well.

Our primary focus during the next meeting will be reviewing Roth conversion
strategies and evaluating potential ways to reduce the long-term impact of
future RMDs and taxation while preserving flexibility and simplicity.

Thank you again for your trust and transparency. We sincerely enjoy working
with thoughtful people like you, and we look forward to continuing the
conversation.

Have a wonderful weekend and enjoy your riding time if the weather
cooperates.

Warm regards,
Ken Ring
Jamie Lippert
Ken Ring
CEO/CPA
Paradigm Capital Group Florida LLC
Colonel(retired), US Army
239-470-8523 cell
messages.subject: Germaine Santon - Thank you and Next Steps
messages.threadKey: email-germaine santon - thank you and next steps

messages.body:
Hello, Ken and Jamie. I am still a tad bit intrigued with that real estate fund to eliminate taxes on my RMDs with business losses against an LLC in my IRA....I told you about this last time we spoke. The LLC for my business would be set up in Florida and is a part of a self-directed IRA. There will be no separate business filings because a self-directed IRA is tax exempt per my contact. This will generate a K-1 loss for my personal filing and I would not have to do a business tax return. I am and still will be researching this...but thought I would pass along this info to you, as well as the attached brochure.  I still feel that it is a bit more complex than I want to get involved in again...KISS (keep it simple, stupid). I am also concerned with finding a good CPA that can handle things. 
I do understand self-directed IRAs a wee bit as I looked into them decades ago. I do know that back then I needed to have some non-Fidelity structure to manage it or contain the money that was self-directed? 
Thanks.  (see attached)Germaine Santon

messages.body:
Germaine,

Thank you for the information and the brochure. We will review this and
discuss it on Friday's call along with our other objectives. Yes,
self-directed IRAs used to be much more difficult to set up legally,
however, with a reputable financial institution that meets the strict
requirements to be a "Third Party Administrator" (TPA) for these accounts,
it has become much more common.

Warm regards - Ken & Jamie

Kenneth E Ring Jr (Ken)
Managing Partner (CEO) | CPA
Certified Tax & Business Advisor (CTBA)
Certified Defense Financial Manager (CDFM)  Level III

> decades ago. I do know that back then I needed to have some non-Fidelity
> structure to manage it or contain the money that was self-directed?
>
> Thanks.  (see attached)
> Germaine Santon
>
